summary: A lovely recording captures a tight second set highlighted by an inspired Playing‑China Doll‑High Time sequence and a standout performance of The Music Never Stopped.
review: Reviewers rave about the second‑set energy, especially the seamless Playing > China Doll > High Time stretch and Jerry’s extended lead on The Music Never Stopped. The sound is described as clear and pleasant, though a few note that the overall show feels a bit patchy. Overall the performance and recording combine to make this a memorable 1984 spring concert.

ai_rating.rationale: Four of five reviewers give high marks (3‑5 stars) and emphasize strong musical moments; recording quality is praised as clear and lovely, yielding a rating above average.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Listeners note the sound is "pretty good" and "lovely," with no major flaws mentioned; no specific source (soundboard/audience) identified.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Second set is repeatedly called "tight" and "beautifully played," while the overall concert is described as a bit patchy by one reviewer.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Played longer than usual on The Music Never Stopped; appeared somber in parts.
band_member_comments.Drums: Jam section created a sense of timelessness for listeners; the Playing‑China Doll‑High Time segment from behind the drums was especially impressive.
